title: MaxPerson
description: MAXGEN v1.3 — Maxwell Genealogy Standard. A probabilistic individual identity synthesized from one or more MaxRecord source documents. No assertion is ever overwritten; new evidence creates new assertions with confidence scores. Symmetric relationships (parent↔child, spouse↔spouse) require atomic bidirectional writes — see SYNC WARNING annotations. v1.3 adds: merge/dedup provenance (merge_history, duplicate_of, merge_status — a merge is a reversible assertion, never a deletion), event_assertions[] for long-tail life events, place_registry[] for gazetteer normalization, and an extensions object for experimental fields.

person_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this person entity.

schema_version.description: MAXGEN version this record conforms to.

is_living.description: True if this person may be alive. Enforces 404 on all open endpoints.

composite_confidence.description: Aggregate confidence that all assertions in this record refer to the same real individual.

name_assertions.description: All known names for this person with individual confidence scores. Multiple names handle maiden names, aliases, spelling variants.

birth_assertions.description: All birth event claims with individual confidence scores.

death_assertions.description: All death event claims with individual confidence scores.

parent_assertions.description: All claimed parent relationships. Multiple entries are normal — confidence scores express certainty, never forcing a single correct answer. BIDIRECTIONAL SYNC REQUIRED — when a parent_assertion is added here referencing parent P, the corresponding child_assertion must be written to P's MaxPerson record atomically. Both writes must succeed or neither should persist.

conflict_flag.description: True if this assertion conflicts with another assertion for the same parent role.

spouse_assertions.description: All claimed spouse relationships. Multiple entries are normal — handles serial marriages, polygamy, and conflicting documentary claims. BIDIRECTIONAL SYNC REQUIRED — marriage is inherently symmetric: when a spouse_assertion is added to person A referencing spouse B, the matching spouse_assertion must be added to B's MaxPerson record atomically (same marriage_year_min/max, same source_record_id). Both writes must succeed or neither should persist. To represent end of marriage (divorce, annulment, death), add an end_year_min/end_year_max to BOTH sides.

end_year_min.description: Year marriage ended (divorce, annulment, or death of spouse). Omit if marriage still in effect at time of last known evidence.

conflict_flag.description: True if this assertion conflicts with another assertion for this spouse pair.

child_assertions.description: All claimed child relationships. BIDIRECTIONAL SYNC REQUIRED — when a child_assertion is added here, the corresponding parent_assertion must be written to the child's Person record atomically. Both writes must succeed or neither should persist.

conflict_flag.description: True if this assertion conflicts with another assertion for this child.

occupation_assertions.description: All known occupations for this person with confidence scores. Used for disambiguation when name and date are insufficient.

external_ids.description: Known identifiers for this person in external systems. Used for cross-source deduplication. Keys are system names (e.g. wikidata_qid, familysearch_pid, findagrave_id, ia_id, ssdi_id); values are the identifier strings. Open-ended to accommodate future sources.

dna_evidence.description: MAXGEN v1.2 — DNA chains that touch this person as a candidate MRCA or direct subject. Each entry is independent evidence that other assertions about this person (parents, birth dates) can use for Bayesian confidence boosting via noisy-OR. SYNC WARNING: each entry corresponds to a row in dna_evidence_chains (Postgres derived table). Updates here must keep dna_id references valid; cascades on consent withdrawal.

dna_id_a.description: First MaxDNA record in the matching pair.

dna_id_b.description: Second MaxDNA record in the matching pair.

path_length_generations.description: Number of generations between the two living testers, summed through this MRCA.

likelihood.description: P(observed shared_cm | this person is the MRCA at this path length).

confidence_delta.description: Per-chain boost applied via noisy-OR. Capped at 0.5 to prevent single-chain overconfidence.

event_assertions.description: MAXGEN v1.3 — Long-tail life events beyond the first-class birth/death/marriage assertions. Add a new event_type here rather than bumping the schema for each new event kind. First-class birth/death remain separate because they are the most queried.

place_registry.description: MAXGEN v1.3 — Normalized gazetteer links for places referenced anywhere in this record's assertions. Resolves free-text place_as_written strings to stable IDs WITHOUT modifying each assertion (avoids a mass backfill when gazetteer integration runs later). Handles historical polities ('Austria-Hungary 1880') via historical_polity + valid-year range. Populated during/after gazetteer integration; may be empty.

place_as_written.description: The exact place string as it appears in assertions; the join key.

historical_polity.description: Sovereign entity at the relevant time (e.g. 'Austria-Hungary', 'Kingdom of Prussia', 'Ottoman Empire').

merge_status.description: MAXGEN v1.3 — Lifecycle state. 'active' = a live distinct entity. 'merged_away' = absorbed into another entity (see duplicate_of); the row is NEVER deleted, preserving append-only history and reversibility. 'split_pending' = flagged for splitting back apart.

duplicate_of.description: MAXGEN v1.3 — When merge_status is 'merged_away', the person_id of the surviving entity this one was absorbed into. Null/absent otherwise. Lets queries follow a merged entity to its survivor and lets a merge be reversed.

merge_history.description: MAXGEN v1.3 — Record of entities absorbed INTO this one. A merge is itself a probabilistic assertion: it has confidence, method, author, and timestamp, and is fully reversible. Nothing is destroyed on merge — the absorbed entity persists with merge_status='merged_away' and duplicate_of pointing here.

merged_person_id.description: The entity that was absorbed into this one.

match_signals.description: What drove the match — e.g. name_similarity, year_overlap, place_match, shared_external_id. Open-ended for scorer flexibility.

reverted_at.description: Set when this merge was undone. Presence means the merge is no longer in effect but the history is retained for audit.

source_record_ids.description: All RawRecord UUIDs that contributed evidence to this Person entity.

extensions.description: MAXGEN v1.3 — Designated namespace for experimental or contributor-specific fields. Because the rest of the schema is additionalProperties:false (strict validation), new ideas go here first without a schema bump or Human Gate. Promote proven fields into the formal schema later.

redistribution_license.description: Most restrictive license among all contributing source records.

asserted_by.description: Agent ID or contributor that created this Person entity.

judge_approved.description: True if the judge-agent has validated this entity's assertions.
